The Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 12101

The Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 2611

.Section 9202 of title 51See Effective Date of 2019 Amendment note below.1

children.heading: Laws which may be made applicable
children.label: b Laws which may be made applicable

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Board shall review provisions of Federal law (including regulations) relating to (A) the terms and conditions of employment (including hiring, promotion, demotion, termination, salary, wages, overtime compensation, benefits, work assignments or reassignments, grievance and disciplinary procedures, protection from discrimination in personnel actions, occupational health and safety, and family and medical and other leave) of employees, and (B) access to public services and accommodations.

children.heading: Board report
children.label: 2 Board report
children.text: Beginning on , and every 2 years thereafter, the Board shall report on (A) whether or to what degree the provisions described in paragraph (1) are applicable or inapplicable to the legislative branch, and (B) with respect to provisions inapplicable to the legislative branch, whether such provisions should be made applicable to the legislative branch. The presiding officers of the House of Representatives and the Senate shall cause each such report to be printed in the Congressional Record and each such report shall be referred to the committees of the House of Representatives and the Senate with jurisdiction.December 31, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: describe the manner in which the provisions of the bill or joint resolution apply to the legislative branch; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a provision not applicable to the legislative branch, include a statement of the reasons the provision does not apply.

children.heading: Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act of 2008
children.label: c Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act of 2008

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The provisions of this chapter that apply to a violation of shall be considered to apply to a violation of title II of the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act of 2008 ( et seq.), consistent with section 207(c) of that Act ().section 1311(a)(1) of this title42 U.S.C. 2000ff42 U.S.C. 2000ff–6(c)

children.heading: No limitation on other laws
children.label: A No limitation on other laws
children.text: Nothing in this section limits the provisions of this chapter that apply to a violation of a law described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Other laws
children.label: B Other laws
children.text: A law described in this subparagraph is a law (even if not listed in subsection (a) or this subsection) that explicitly applies one or more provisions of this chapter to a violation.
